summary: Wrap Surf's Chrome-fingerprinted transport with MFPTransport in newHTTPClient so all spec-emitted commands clear MFP's anti-bot.
reason: MFP redirects stdlib User-Agent requests to /account/login even with valid cookies. Surf's TLS fingerprint matches a real Chrome and clears the challenge; MFPTransport must remain as the outer wrapper for /v2/ host rewriting and mfp-* headers.
validated_outcome: Live diary get-day, water, exercise, food search, and user auth-token all return real account data instead of the login-page redirect they hit before.
